graphEvents.label: P1 non-active tracked DBs skip TRUNCATE checkpoint

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Correctness/data-safety sweep spans cache migration and eviction, SQLite WAL durability across canonical/shard schemas, daemon shutdown ordering, retention maintenance, and embed batching behavior.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the under-explored focus areas named in the iteration prompt, plus directly relevant tests for checkpoint order, byte chunking, and retention maintenance.

findingDetails.title: Non-active tracked SQLite connections skip the TRUNCATE checkpoint on shutdown

searchLedger.invariant: Cache eviction should enforce global, per-profile, and query byte budgets while preserving shard-qualified table access.

searchActions.result: cacheTable switches to active_vec when attached; migration, lookup recency, store, and LRU byte-budget deletion all use cacheTable(db).
Tests cover fresh schema, legacy backfill, scoped lookup, global/profile/query byte caps, shrink_memory, and legacy lookup compatibility.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cache eviction or shard-table migration defect found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Reviewed implementation and targeted tests cover the named byte-budget/LRU and shard-qualified table behavior.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Shutdown may apply the TRUNCATE checkpoint sequence only to the active singleton, leaving prior tracked custom DB connections to detach/close without the explicit durability guarantee.

searchActions.result: initialize_db(custom_path) creates tracked connections; close_db loops all tracked conns but only checkpoints the active db after non-active conns are closed.
Existing tests assert active DB and active shard checkpointing, and separately exercise switching active DBs, but do not assert checkpointing for every tracked connection on shutdown.

searchLedger.rationale: The same close path that explicitly rejects passive close as insufficient for the active DB relies on passive close for every non-active tracked DB.

searchLedger.invariant: Fatal shutdown should drain file-watcher work before closing the vector DB so drains cannot reopen DB after the TRUNCATE checkpoint.

searchActions.result: fatalShutdown awaits fileWatcher.close before vectorIndex.closeDb and documents the drain-before-close invariant.
file-watcher close path tracks in-flight tasks and rejects queued work during close, matching the shutdown drain design.
Test asserts vectorIndex.closeDb appears after the fileWatcher drain in fatalShutdown.

searchLedger.rationale: The daemon-level ordering invariant is present and covered; the new WAL finding is confined to vector-index-store's handling of multiple tracked DB connections.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: fatalShutdown drains before closeDb and has direct source-order coverage.

searchLedger.invariant: Retention deletes should run FTS optimize and guarded incremental vacuum/checkpoint maintenance after the delete transaction.

searchActions.result: post-delete maintenance runs after sweepTx, optimizes FTS, checks auto_vacuum before incremental_vacuum, and truncates WAL best-effort.
Tests assert optimize/auto_vacuum/wal_checkpoint run outside the transaction and are skipped when no rows are deleted.

searchLedger.rationale: No new retention sweep correctness defect found.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The requested post-delete FTS optimize and incremental_vacuum guards are implemented and tested.

searchLedger.invariant: Embedding batches should chunk by count and byte budget, preserve output order/null slots, retry only bounded transient readiness failures, and adopt custom dimensions consistently.

searchActions.result: Router chunks while respecting both SPECKIT_EMBED_CLIENT_MAX_BATCH and MAX_EMBED_REQUEST_BYTES before calling provider.embedBatch.
HF local embedPrepared uses two attempts for retryable readiness errors, validates row count and dimensions, and embedBatch preserves null slots for skipped inputs.
Tests cover byte-budget splitting, batched POST row counts, null-slot preservation, truncated response rejection, and one-time dimension adoption for custom models.

searchLedger.rationale: No new batching/concurrency defect found in the reviewed client/server embedding path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Implementation and tests cover the requested count+byte chunking, bounded retry, and dimension adoption behavior.

title: Non-active tracked SQLite connections skip the TRUNCATE checkpoint on shutdown

claim: close_db() can leave WAL frames for non-active tracked SQLite connections because it closes them before running the explicit main/shard TRUNCATE checkpoint sequence.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether every tracked connection receives a checkpoint helper, whether the non-active branch calls checkpointAllWal(), and whether test coverage asserts checkpointing after switching active databases. The reviewed code only checkpoints the active singleton, and the multi-database test checks cache isolation rather than shutdown durability.
alternativeExplanation: better-sqlite3 may passively checkpoint some frames on close(), but the active path's own comment rejects passive close as sufficient for the FTS/WAL corruption-prevention invariant.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if db_connections is proven test-only or if better-sqlite3 close is documented and tested to fully truncate both main and attached shard WALs for these non-active connections under the deployed runtime.

direction: cache eviction and shard-qualified cache migration
reason: cacheTable(db), migration, LRU byte budgets, recency updates, and direct tests cover the reviewed behavior
fatalShutdown awaits fileWatcher.close before vectorIndex.closeDb and has source-order test coverage

direction: retention post-delete optimize and vacuum guards
reason: post-delete FTS optimize, auto_vacuum guarded incremental_vacuum, and WAL checkpoint run after the delete transaction and are tested

direction: embed batching count/byte chunking and hf-local retry/dimension adoption
reason: router byte/count chunking and hf-local bounded retry, row count validation, null-slot preservation, and custom dimension adoption are implemented and covered by tests

event.label: P1 non-active tracked DBs skip TRUNCATE checkpoint
